headline: Twenty-nine kept. One added.
standfirst: Two-month per-keyword view across the engagement period. Every keyword the site ranked for in May still ranks today. The brand term locked at #1. PAMM-related terms climbed inside their existing top-100 positions. One net new keyword added. Each of the 27 commercial-intent keywords currently off-index now has its on-page foundation work deployed, ready for the next reindex cycle.

compare_hero.title: Tracked keywords ranking in top 100
compare_hero.subtitle: Foundation rebuild without losing rank — and one new keyword in
compare_hero.caption: Plus zero keywords lost rank during the same period. Twenty-eight came in, twenty-eight kept their position, one new term added. The first weekly increase since February.

deliverables.title: Foundation deployed for the off-index keyword recovery
deliverables.subtitle: Each commercial keyword's landing page now has the schema and structural signals it was missing

rows.why: Page now eligible for FAQ rich-results when keywords like 'metatrader4 platform', 'mt4 forex brokers' are searched.
Page now eligible for FAQ rich-results when 'metatrader 5 webtrader', 'mt5 platform', 'mt5 trading' are searched.
Targeted at 'forex trading uae', 'forex trading brokers', 'online forex trading platforms' — top recovery candidates.
Two pages, 18 combined Q&As targeting 'funded trading account', 'proprietary trading', 'what is prop trading' — the highest-volume recovery cluster.

rows.metric: FAQ schema on 6 other commercial pages
rows.why: stock-cfds-trading, indices-trading, commodities-trading, social-trading, cms-webtrader, cpay — each gets dedicated FAQ schema for its keyword cluster.
EN, AR, FA versions stopped competing with each other for the same query. Each language gets credit for its own keyword.

prose.title: Twenty-nine kept. One added. Twenty-seven primed.
paragraphs: Two-month per-keyword view: of the 28 keywords ranking in May, all 28 still rank in June at the same or better position. The brand term cms prime locked at #1. PAMM account moved from #27 to #25. One new keyword entered the top 100. Zero declines.

The 27 off-index commercial keywords haven't yet appeared in ranking columns — but every single one's landing page now has the FAQ schema, hreflang, and enriched Organization markup it was missing. Coming into May, those pages had zero structured signals to compete with. As of June 1, they have everything Google needs to evaluate them properly on the next crawl.

The July 1 reading is the first window where the foundation work shows up in position columns. For now, what we can show is the line we held: 28 keywords in, 29 keywords out, zero slipped, foundation deployed across the entire recovery pipeline.

pull_quote.text: Twenty-eight kept their position. One new keyword in. Twenty-seven landing pages now have the foundation they need.
